chunks.page_summary: This page provides a quick guide for Modbus TCP verification on Rheonics SME devices using Modbus Poll master software, including default network settings and initial connection steps.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Quick Modbus TCP verification using Modbus Poll master software

Modified on: Thu, 29 Feb, 2024 at 9:47 PM

ModbusPoll is a commercially available software from Witte GmbH.

1. Intro to Modbus TCP/IP on SME (Rheonics Sensor Electronics)

All SME devices implement a MODBUS TCP/IP protocol. The Rheonics SME device allows the configuration of the Ethernet IP address, mask, gateway and DNS using the RCP software. Please refer to the RCP software manual for instructions on setting these parameters. The factory default values are shown below.

DHCP = True
IP address=0.0.0.0
Mask= 0.0.0.0
Gateway = 0.0.0.0
DNS=0.0.0.0
TCP Port= 502
Slave ID = 255

NOTE: The default TCP Port and slave ID can't be modified by the user. There is no need to configure the port in RCP since by default is 502

2. Intro to Modbuspoll tool TCP/IP

2.1. Click the “Connection” tab and select “Connect”.
key_facts: ModbusPoll is commercially available software from Witte GmbH.
All Rheonics SME devices implement a MODBUS TCP/IP protocol.
Rheonics SME devices allow configuration of Ethernet IP address, mask, gateway, and DNS using the RCP software.
The factory default values for SME devices include DHCP = True, IP address = 0.0.0.0, Mask = 0.0.0.0, Gateway = 0.0.0.0, DNS = 0.0.0.0, TCP Port = 502, and Slave ID = 255.
To use the ModbusPoll tool, the user should click the "Connection" tab and select "Connect".
warnings_notes: NOTE: The default TCP Port and slave ID cannot be modified by the user.
There is no need to configure the port in RCP since it is 502 by default.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions and screenshots for configuring a Modbus TCP/IP connection within the Modbus Poll (Mbpoll1) software, detailing communication parameters and server settings.
chunks.extracted_text:
Application: Modbus Poll - Mbpoll1
Menu Bar: File, Edit, Connection, Setup, Functions, Display, View, Window, Help
Toolbar elements: (Icons for save, open, print, cut, copy, paste, undo, redo, delete, connect, disconnect, etc. plus numerical indicators: 05, 06, 15, 16, 17, 22, 23, TC)

Status Bar (Top): Mbpoll1, Tx = 0: Err = 0: ID = 1: F = 03: SR = 1000ms, No connection

Connection Setup Dialog:
  Title: Connection Setup
  Buttons: OK, Cancel
  Fields:
    Alias: 00000
    Connection Type: Modbus TCP/IP
    Serial Settings:
      Communications Port: COM4
      Baud: 38400
      Data bits: 8
      Parity: Odd
      Stop Bit: 1
      Advanced... (button)
    Mode: RTU (selected), ASCII
    Response Timeout: 3000 [ms]
    Delay Between Polls: 1000 [ms]
    Remote Modbus Server:
      IP Address or Node Name: 192.168.8.133
      Server Port: 502
      Connect Timeout: 3000 [ms]
      IP Version: IPv4 (selected), IPv6

Instruction 2.2: With the new menu open, select the connection for Modbus TCP/IP
Figure Caption 1: Figure 1. Open the Connection TAB

Status Bar (Bottom): For Help, press F1. [192.168.8.133]: 502
Instruction 2.3: Fill every parameter as shown in the red boxes:
Figure Caption 2: Figure 2. Setting in Modbus TCP/IP
key_facts: The Modbus Poll software (Mbpoll1) is used for Modbus communication.
To configure a Modbus TCP/IP connection, the user must open the 'Connection Setup' dialog.
The connection type should be set to 'Modbus TCP/IP'.
Serial communication parameters like Baud Rate (38400), Data bits (8), Parity (Odd), and Stop Bits (1) are configurable.
The Modbus mode is set to RTU.
Response Timeout and Delay Between Polls are configured to 3000 ms and 1000 ms respectively.
The remote Modbus server's IP address is 192.168.8.133 and the server port is 502.
The connection timeout for the server is set to 3000 ms, using IPv4.

key_facts: The document provides instructions for configuring Modbus TCP/IP communication settings.
The Modbus TCP/IP server settings include an IP address of 192.168.8.133, server port 502, and connect timeout of 3000 ms.
Serial settings are specified as 38400 Baud, 8 data bits, odd parity, and 1 stop bit, with RTU mode selected.
The Modbus client software shows response timeout of 10000 ms and delay between polls of 20 ms.
Users are instructed to navigate to the 'Setup' tab and select 'Read/Write Definition' within the Modbus Poll software.

key_facts: The 'Read/Write Definition' dialog configures Modbus communication parameters.
The Modbus communication is set to read input registers (Function 04) with a Slave ID of 255, starting at address 40, and reading 10 registers.
The scan rate for Modbus communication is set to 1000 milliseconds.
The displayed data in the Modbus Poll table for addresses 40 to 49 corresponds to parameter 0.
The Modbus Poll status indicates 1 transmission, 0 errors, Slave ID 255, Function 04, and a Scan Rate of 1000ms.
The Modbus client is connected to IP address 192.168.8.133 on port 502.

The table 'Parameter Address Input Registers' lists various process parameters including viscosity, density, temperature, and kinematic viscosity, along with their median, integer, status, and uncalibrated values.
Values are provided for parameters like Viscosity Median (0.523596), Density Median (0.53), Temperature Median (16.84), and Kinematic Viscosity (0.987916).
diagram_labels: Figure 5. Set read/write function for Modbus TCP/IP

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to correct data types and endianness for Modbus registers using the Mbpoll1 software by demonstrating menu options for data formatting.
chunks.extracted_text:
3. Correcting data types of registers and endianness

3.1. Right-click in the parameter to fix a value that is readable, a special menu is going to appear. Select “Format”

Figure 7. Menu editor for data format

Modbus Poll - Mbpoll1
File Edit Connection Setup Functions Display View Window Help
Mbpoll1
Tx = 16; Err = 0; ID = 255; F = 04; SR = 1000ms
Alias | 00040
0: 40 = 016133
1: 41 = 10859
2: 42 = 00052
3: 43 = 000256
4: 44 = 015702
5: 45 = 008840
6: 46 = 000001
7: 47 = 000000
8: 48 = 016135
9: 49 = -20972

Context Menu options (Figure 7):
Format >
Read/write Definition... F8
Cut Ctrl+X
Copy Ctrl+C
Paste Ctrl+V
Select All Ctrl+A
Colors... Alt+Shift+C
Font... Alt+Shift+F

3.2. Pay attention to the correct format of each parameter

Figure 8. All options for data display

Modbus Poll - Mbpoll1
File Edit Connection Setup Functions Display View Window Help
Mbpoll1
Tx = 19; Err = 0; ID = 255; F = 04; SR = 1000ms
Alias | 00040
0: 40 = 016133
1: 41 = 10859
2: 42 = 00052
3: 43 = 000256
4: 44 = 015702
5: 45 = 008840
6: 46 = 000001
7: 47 = 000000
8: 48 = 016135
9: 49 = -20972

Context Menu options (Figure 8, with Format submenu open):
Format >
  ✓ Signed Alt+Shift
  Unsigned Alt+Shift
  Hex - ASCII Alt+Shift
  Binary Alt+Shift
  32 Bit Signed
  32 Bit Unsigned
  64 Bit Signed
  64 Bit Unsigned
  32 Bit Float
  64 Bit Double
Read/write Definition... F8
Cut Ctrl+X
Copy Ctrl+C
Paste Ctrl+V
Select All Ctrl+A
Colors... Alt+Shift+C
Font... Alt+Shift+F

3.3. In the sample above, the address 40 corresponds to the following format (Float 32, Depending on the endianness of the host it might be necessary to swap high/low byte and/or the respective registers.)
key_facts: Users can correct data types and endianness of Modbus registers using the Modbus Poll (Mbpoll1) software.
Accessing data format options involves right-clicking on a parameter and selecting 'Format' from the context menu.
The Modbus Poll software offers various data display formats including Signed, Unsigned, Hex-ASCII, Binary, 32 Bit Signed/Unsigned/Float, and 64 Bit Signed/Unsigned/Double.
For the sample given, Modbus address 40 is interpreted as a Float 32.
The software connection parameters are Tx=19, Err=0, ID=255, F=04, and SR=1000ms (as seen in Figure 8).
warnings_notes: Depending on the endianness of the host it might be necessary to swap high/low byte and/or the respective registers.

key_facts: The Modbus Poll software is used to configure data types for Modbus communication.
Users can select various data formats including signed, unsigned, Hex-ASCII, Binary, and different bit sizes (32-bit, 64-bit) for integers and floating-point numbers.
The configuration supports different endianness options (Big-endian, Little-endian, and byte swap variations) for 32-bit Float values.
Rheonics provides support articles for Modbus Input Registers and pre-configured Modbuspoll files for their devices.
A 64-bit version of the Modbuspoll tool is available for download via Dropbox.
The Modbus Poll application shows a scan rate (SR) of 1000ms.
